title: service-accounts — HTTP routes
description: Nine routes total. Eight on the platform_admin plane at `/api/v1/platform/service-accounts/*` (super_admin/security/ops-scoped for writes; wider read). One unauthenticated route at `/api/v1/service-accounts/token` for credential exchange (rate-limited per-SA + per-IP-/24). No tenant-plane surface — tenants never see SA rows even for their own machine credentials.

groups.name: service-accounts-crud
groups.description: Primary CRUD + lifecycle surface for machine credentials. platform.mfa_required + platform.audit inherited from the PlatformUser blueprint's guard-level defaults — the module composes them here rather than replacing.

routes.description: List. Filters: filter[application_id], filter[tenant_id], filter[is_enabled], filter[has_expired], filter[dormant_days], filter[signer_kid]. Search: q (matches name + description). Default sort: created_at desc. Excludes soft-deleted rows unless include_deleted=true. Redacted list — no secret_hash, no last_used_ip.

Provision a new SA. Body: CreateServiceAccountInput. Response: ServiceAccountCreatedResponse (includes the plaintext secret ONCE). Fires ServiceAccountCreated on success. Refuses with 422 on invalid application_id / tenant_id / signer_kid / name.

Detail. Includes application_slug, tenant_slug, signer_kid, expires_at, secret_rotated_at, last_used_at, last_used_ip (visible to platform-admin), status, computed accessors (days_until_expiry, is_dormant, is_expiring_soon).

Update mutable fields (name, description, is_enabled, expires_at, metadata). Refuses with 422 SERVICE_ACCOUNT_IMMUTABLE_FIELD on any attempt to change application_id / tenant_id / signer_kid. Refuses expires_at values in the past.
Soft-delete + disable. Fires ServiceAccountDeleted(erasure_phase='soft_delete'). Starts the retention hold clock (default 90d).
Rotate the SA secret. Body: { ttl_days?: int, reason: string }. Response: ServiceAccountCreatedResponse (same shape as create — includes plaintext secret ONCE). Fires ServiceAccountSecretRotated.

Issue a test JWT with configurable scope + TTL. Body: IssueTestJwtInput. Response: TokenExchangeResponse (same shape as the public exchange endpoint). Fires ServiceAccountJwtIssued with trigger='test'. Refuses when SA is disabled or expired.

Usage report over the window. Query params: window (24h | 7d | 30d | 90d; default 30d), format (json | csv). Response: ServiceAccountUsageReportData. Every invocation writes its own audit row so 'who ran a report on which SA' is auditable.

groups.name: service-accounts-token-exchange
groups.description: Public, unauthenticated credential-exchange endpoint. Rate-limited per-SA (5/min) + per-IP-/24 (20/min). No auth guard — the SA authenticates itself by presenting its bcrypt secret. Every failure fires ServiceAccountFingerprinter observation for anomaly detection.

routes.description: Exchange bcrypt secret for HS256 JWT. Body: TokenExchangeInput. Response: TokenExchangeResponse. Rate-limit rejections return 429 SERVICE_ACCOUNT_RATE_LIMITED with Retry-After. Credential-verification rejections return 401 INVALID_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_CREDENTIALS (generic; no enumeration signal). Successful exchange fires ServiceAccountJwtIssued with trigger='exchange'.

boundary_note: The token-exchange endpoint is INTENTIONALLY on the public host set (available on tenant subdomains + platform-admin + central) so a machine deployed inside any Stackra-facing zone can reach it. It does NOT require the caller to route through the internal-service mesh (that would break bootstrap sequencing — the first inter-service call has to happen SOMEHOW). The security posture is enforced by: (a) aggressive rate limiting, (b) generic error responses (enumeration safety), (c) fingerprint-based anomaly detection with critical-queue notifications on rate spikes + novel tuples.

identity/auth: consumes ServiceAccountJwtIssuerInterface + delegates JWT signing to auth::JwtSigner
seeds the default HS256 kid at boot so this module's health probe passes
